title: Topology Declarations, Property Equivalence, Policies, and Ownership · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A messaging pairing room predicts publisher, exchange, queue, consumer, ownership, and failure transitions before assembling the first versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest for topology declarations, property equivalence, policies, and ownership. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams.
Make repeated declarations equivalent in type, durability, exclusivity, auto-delete, and immutable arguments and diagnose precondition mismatch.
Choose durable, exclusive server-named, auto-delete, and queue-TTL lifecycles from responsibility and race evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Topology Owner Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 5301 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to protect property equivalence and route mutable operations through policy or versioned migration; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Topology Owner Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Topology Owner Contract, keep Topology Owner Contract connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Topology Migration
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 1349 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to protect property equivalence and route mutable operations through policy or versioned migration; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Topology Migration.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Topology Migration, keep Topology Migration connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Topology Migration symptom in versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest during this workshop case.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: RabbitMQ Exchanges and Queues — Controls exchange types, default and alternate exchanges, routing, queue names, properties, durability, ordering, arguments, limits, and queue choice.

stimulus.title: Topology Migration failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Redeploying all services simultaneously makes destructive queue changes atomic.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Topology Migration condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Topology Migration output and hide the violated versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Topology Migration.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Topology Migration without its stated preconditions, omit the versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Topology Migration for versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Workshop Topology Migration
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Topology Migration: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop messaging team handles case 8730 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to protect property equivalence and route mutable operations through policy or versioned migration; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then raise a broker resource alarm and stop new publisher admission. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Workshop Topology Migration.
steps.why: Retrieving Topology Migration through the concrete versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Workshop Topology Migration, keep Topology Migration connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback.
